message:
fix(database): remove dead code, fix pool lock discipline, apply clippy idioms

MySQL (mysql.rs):
  Removed four unused methods from PacketBuffer and one unused field
  from Handshake:
  - PacketBuffer::clear() — never called after initial parse phase
  - PacketBuffer::write_u16_le() — only u32_le is used in practice
  - PacketBuffer::write_lenenc_str() — auth uses raw byte writes
  - Handshake::protocol_version — parsed but never read

Pool (pool.rs):
  - Added explicit drop(inner) after releasing the pool's Mutex guard
    in two hot paths within get(): after popping a connection and in
    the connect-failure rollback. The Mutex guard was previously held
    across the entire connection attempt, blocking other callers.
  - ensure_min_idle: simplified match to if-let for the connect path
  - Debug impls for DbPool and AsyncDbPool: changed .finish() to
    .finish_non_exhaustive() since not all fields are shown

Postgres (postgres.rs):
  Clippy-driven idiom improvements throughout:
  - FromSql impls: i16/i32/i64/f32/f64 use Self::from_be_bytes instead
    of the concrete type name
  - String::from_sql: uses std::string::ToString::to_string method ref
  - Vec<u8>::from_sql: bytea hex path uses strip_prefix with map_or_else
  - Option<T>::type_oid: uses ToSql::type_oid method ref
  No behavioral changes.

SQLite (sqlite.rs):
  Single-line addition (newline at end of file).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
